Umbiko uthumela umyalezo ekwesabeni izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo,okuthatha okukhulu nokunyuka ezimpilweni zabantu,ukuphila,izinhlelo zempilo ,imiphakathi ezomnotho nemiphakathi," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Izimemezelo zombiko zokunyuka okungagcineka kutshalomali lwezempilo ukubuyela esimweni seSustainable Development Goals."
translations.hypothesis: यद्यपि, महामारीले धेरै स्वास्थ्य-संबंधित मापदण्डहरूले अझमा बिच, र उच्च-गुणवत्ताको स्वास्थ्य सेवा, नियमित इम्युनाइजेसन र आर्थिक सुरक्षाको उपलब्धतामा असमानता, योगदान गरेको छ। फलस्वरूप, मलेरिया र टीबीमा सुधारको प्रवृत्ति उल्टाउन भयो, र धेरै व्यक्तिहरूले उपेक्षित उष्णकटिबंधीय रोगहरू (NTDs)का लागि उपचार प्राप्त गर्न सकेन्। "जागतिक स्वास्थ्य तथ्याङ्क" ले, विश्वको स्वास्थ्य अवस्थाको वार्षिक मूल्याङ्कन प्रदान गर्दछ। रिपोर्टले गैर-संक्रामक रोगहरूको खतरामा स्पष्ट सन्देश दिन्छ, जुन जीवन, आजीविका, स्वास्थ्य प्रणाली, समुदाय, अर्थव्यवस्था र समाजहरूमा भारी र क्रमिक प्रभाव पार्छ," भन्यो डा. टेड्रोस एधानोम गेब्रेयसस, विश्व स्वास्थ्य संगठनका निर्देशक-सामान्य। "रिपोर्टले, दिगो विकास लक्ष्यहरूमा फर्किन, स्वास्थ्य र स्वास्थ्य प्रणालीमा पर्याप्त लगानीको आह्वान गर्दछ।"

translations.source: NCDs ̶ an ever-increasing health threat for future generationsDespite overall health progress, the share of deaths caused annually by NCDs has grown consistently and is now claiming nearly three quarters of all lives lost each year. If this trend continues, NCDs are projected to account for about 86% of the 90 million annual deaths by mid-century; consequently, 77 million of these will be due to NCDs – a nearly 90% increase in absolute numbers since 2019. Stagnating progress calls for acceleration More recent trends show signs of slowdown in the annual rate of reduction (ARR) for many indicators. For example, the global maternal mortality ratio needs to decline by 11.6% per year between 2021 and 2030 to meet the SDG target. Similarly, the net reduction in TB incidence from 2015 to 2021 was only one-fifth of the way to the 2025 milestone of WHO’s End TB Strategy.
translations.reference: Ama-NCD-ahlezi enyusa ukwesabela ezempilo zesizukulwane sakusasa, Ngaphandle kwakho konke ukuqhubeka, ukwabelana ngokufa okudalwe ngonyaka amaNCD sekukhule njalo futhi manje sekuthathe amakota amathathu azo zonke izimpilo ezilahlekile unyaka ngamunye. Uma lokhu kujwayeleka kuqhubeka, amaNCD azothatha icala le-86% wokufa kwezigidi ezingu-90 ngonyaka phakathi kweminyaka eyikhulu, ngokulandelana izigidi ezingu-77 zalokhu ngezeNCD -kungafinyelela ku-90% ukwenyuka kwezinamba kusukela ngo-2019. Ukuhamba kancane kokuqhubeka kwezimemezelo zokuqhubeka okudumile kwakamuva kukhombisa izinkomba zokuhamba kancane kwezinga lonyaka lokwehla kwezinkomba eziningi (ARR). Isibonelo isilinganiso sokufa komama emhlabeni sidinga ukwehla ngo-11.6% ngonykaka phakathi kuka-2021 no-2030 ukuhlangabezana nokuqondwe yiSDG. Ngokufanayo, ukwehla konke endabeni yeTB kusuka ngo-2015 kuya ku-2021 kwakungamakota amahlanu kosekuhanjiwe ngo-2025 kweWHO END TB Strategy.
translations.hypothesis: रोगहरू – एक लगातार न्वाकल खतराको लागि भविष्यका पुताहरू।यद्यपि समग्र स्वास्थ्यमा प्रगति, हिस्सा ज्यान मार्ने, जसमा NCDs समावेश छन्, लगातार बढेको छ, र अब लगभग तीन चौथाई सबै ज्यान मार्ने प्रति वर्ष। यदि यो प्रवृत्ति जारी रहन्छ, NCDs प्रक्षेपित छन् 90 मिलियन वार्षिक ज्यानमध्ये लगभग 86%; फलस्वरूप, 77 मिलियन इनमध्ये NCDs-ले योगदान गर्ने - लगभग 90% वृद्धे, 2019 देखिको संख्या। स्थिर प्रगतिले गतिमा आवश्यकता। हालका प्रवृत्तियाँले, धेरै मापदण्डहरूमा वार्षिक कमी दर (ARR) मा कमीको चिन्ह देखाउँछन्। उदाहरणको लागि, विश्वव्यापी मातृ ज्यान मार्ने दर, 2021 र 2030 बीच, 11.6% प्रति वर्ष घट्नु आवश्यक छ, ताकि SDG लक्ष्य हासिल भइन्। समान रूपमा, 2015 देखि 2021 सम्म, क्षयरोगको कुल कमी, WHO ले End TB Strategy को 2025 लक्ष्यमा आधामात्र पुग्यो।

translations.source: Despite a reduction in exposure to many health risks – such as tobacco use, alcohol consumption, violence, unsafe water and sanitation, and child stunting – progress was inadequate and exposure to some risks such as air pollution remains high. Alarmingly, the prevalence of obesity is rising with no immediate sign of reversal. Furthermore, expanded access to essential health services has slowed compared to pre-2015 gains, coupled with no significant progress in reducing financial hardship due to health-care costs. This drastically limits our ability to achieve Universal Health Coverage by 2030. “The COVID-19 pandemic is an important reminder that progress is neither linear nor guaranteed,” warns Dr Samira Asma, WHO Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact.
translations.reference: Ngaphandle kokwehla ekubonakaleni kwengcuphe yezempilo eningi-njengokusetshenziswa kwagwayi, ukuphuza utshwala, udlame,amanzi angaphephile nokuhlanzeka, okwezingane, ukuqhubeka nokunganeli nokuvuleleka kwezinye izingcuphe njengokungcola komoya kuhlezi kuphezulu. Okuthusayo, ukusabalala kokukhuluphala ngokweqile kuyanyuka kungekho nophawu lokukubuyisela emuva. Ukuqhubekaukwanda kokuthola emisebenzini yezempilo ebalulekile sekwehlile uma uqhathanisa kokuzuza kwaphambili kwa-2015, okuhlanganiswe nokuqhubeka okukhulu ekwehliseni ubunzima kwezezimali ngenxa yokubiza kokunakekelwa kwezempilo. Imikhawulo emikhulu yokukwazi kwethu ukuthola i-Universal Health Coverage ngo-2030. "Ubhubhane lweCOVID-19 luyisikhumbuzo esibalulekile sokuthi ukuqhubeka akuhambi ngolayini noma akuqinisekisiwe," kuxwayisa uDkt Samira Asma, Umsizi woMqondisi Jikele weMininingo kuWHO, ukuhlaziya nokulethwa kwemithelela.
translations.hypothesis: यद्यपि जोखिमहरू, जस्तै धूम्रपान, मद्यपान, हिंसा, असुरक्षित पानी र सानिटरी, र बच्चाहरूको विकास,ลดลง – तर प्रगति अद्यागेशेन र केही जोखिम, जस्तै वायु प्रदूषण, अڃै उच्च रहन्छ। चिन्ताजनक रूपमा, लतको दर बढिरहेको छ, र यसको समाधान नभएको छ। थप, पहुँचमा सुधार भइको छ, तर यसले स्वास्थ्य सेवाको लागतले निम्त्याइएको आर्थिक कठिनाई कम गर्नमा महत्त्वपूर्ण प्रगति देखिएको छैन। यसले हाम्रो क्षमतालाई 2030 सम्ममा सार्वभौमिक स्वास्थ्य सेवा हासिल गर्नमा सानিয়ে। "COVID-19 महामारीले यो स्पष्ट पार्छ कि प्रगति लगातार र निश्चित छैन," चेतावनी दिन्छ डा. सामिरा अस्मा, WHO डेटा, विश्लेषण र प्रभावको लागि सहायक निर्देशक।

translations.source: “To stay on track towards the 2030 SDG agenda, we must act decisively and collectively to deliver a measurable impact in all countries.” This year’s report includes for the first time a dedicated section on climate change and health, and we anticipate that this will be of more relevance in the report going forward. For this issue and all other areas timely, reliable and disaggregated data are critical to track progress and improve national and global health policies. Editor’s note: The World Health Statistics report is the World Health Organization’s (WHO) annual compilation of the most recent available data on health and health-related indicators. For inquiries, contact [email protected]
translations.reference: "Ukuhlala esimweni okuya kuAjenda yeSDG 2030, kumele sinqume kanhle silethe ngokubambisana kumthelela okaliwe kuwona wonke amazwe." Umbiko wakulonyaka ufaka okokuqala isahluko sokuzinikela kwezokuguquka kwesimo sezulu nezempilo, futhi siyakubona lokhu kuzohlobana embikweni kusuka manje kuya phambili. Ngalenkinga nazo zonke izindawo,ukwethembeka kanye nokungesona isilinganisso solwazi kubucayi ukulandelela ukuqhubeka nokwenza ngcono ezweni nakuzinqubomgomo zezempilo emhlabeni. Inothi lomhleli: iWolrd Health Statistics umbiko yiWolrd Health Organisation (WHO) ukuhlanganisa konyaka kolwazi olukhona lwakamuva kwezempilo kanye nezinkomba ezihlobene nezempilo. Uma unemibuzo xhumana [imeyili ivikelekile]
translations.hypothesis: “यद्यपि हामी चाहन्छौँ हासिल गर्न 2030 SDG, हामी अवश्य लாய्गो र सामूहिक रूपमा कार्य गर्नुपर्छ, ताकि हामी हासिल गर्न सकू। यस वर्षको रिपोर्टले, पहिलोपटक, जलवायु परिवर्तन र स्वास्थ्यको विशिष्ट अनुभाग समावेश, र हामी अपेक्षा छ कि यो रिपोर्ट भविष्यमा अझै धेरै महत्त्वपूर्ण हुनु पर्छ। यस विषय र अन्य क्षेत्रहरूमा, समयबद्ध, विश्वसनीय र विस्तृत जानकारी आवश्यक छ, ताकि हामी प्रगति मापन गर्न र राष्ट्रल र विश्वव्यापी स्वास्थ्य नीजि सुधार गर्न सकू। टिप्पणी: विश्व स्वास्थ्य तथ्यांक रिपोर्ट, विश्व स्वास्थ्य सङ्घ (WHO) ले प्रकाशित गरेको, स्वास्थ्य र स्वास्थ्य-संबंधित मापदण्डहरूमा हालको उपलब्ध जानकारीको वार्षिक संकलन। जानकारीको लागि, कृपया [email protected]

translations.source: WHO Director-General announces Global Health Leaders Awards Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot awarded for lifelong commitment to health WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus will present two lifetime achievement awards to recognize outstanding contributions to advancing global health during the high-level opening session of the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly. The Director-General’s Global Leaders Awards, which were established in 2019, will go to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot. Both recipients are distinguished scientists and public health leaders who were closely involved in the discovery of Ebola virus disease before advancing to leadership positions in global health.
translations.reference: Umqondisi Jikelele weWHO umemezele Imiklomelo yaBaholi beZempilo eMhlabeni UJean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum noPeter Piot baklonyelwe ngokuzibophezela impilo yabo yonke kwezempilo Umqondisi Jikelele weWHO uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus uzokwethula imiklomelo yempilo yonke emibili ukubona ukunikela okumangazayo ukwenza ngcono ezempilo zomhlaba ngesikhathi seseshini yokuvula esezingeni eliphakeme yoMhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha. Imiklomelo yaBaholi boMhlaba yaMqondisi Jikelele, eyasungulwa ngo-2019 izoya kuJean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum noPeter Piot. Bobabili abatholile bangosayensi abahlukile futhi bangabaholi bezempilo yomphakathi ababezibandakanye kakhulu ekutholeni isifo segciwane ibhola ngaphambi kokudlulela phambili ngesikhundla kwezobuholi kwezempilo emhlabeni.
translations.hypothesis: अध्यक्ष जेनेरल WHO, Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum र Peter Piot, घोषित गरिन् "पुरस्कार ल्वाटर ग्लोबल हेल्थ लिडर"। अध्यक्ष जेनेरल WHO, डा. टेड्रोस अधानोम गेब्रेयसस, 76औं विश्व स्वास्थ्य सभाको उच्च-स्तरीय उद्घाटन सत्रमा, उत्कृष्ट योगदानहरूलाई पहिचान गर्न, दुई पुरस्कार प्रदान गर्ने। "पुरस्कार ल्वाटर ग्लोबल हेल्थ लिडर", मार्मिक 2019, पुग्नेछ क Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum र Peter Piot। दुवै प्राप्तकर्ता हुन्, उत्कृष्ट वैज्ञानिक र सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य नेता, जे आउनमा महत्त्वपूर्ण भूमिका खेले, अघि अघि, नेतृत्व भूमिकामा।

translations.source: The award honours their lifetime achievements in public health. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is being recognized for his innovation, leadership and ground-breaking contributions to the management and treatment of diseases such as Ebola virus disease. He has played a critical role in improving health outcomes in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the African region, and the global community. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the Director General of the DRC’s National Institute of Biomedical Research in Kinshasa, full Professor of Microbiology at the University of Kinshasa Medical School and the inaugural president of the Congolese Academy of Science. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum was part of the team that discovered the Zaïre ebolavirus during the first recorded outbreak in 1976, in Equateur Province, Central DRC.
translations.reference: Umklomelo ubahlonipha ngabakuzuze impilo yabo yonke kwezempilo yomphakathi. USolwazi Muyembe-Tamfum waseDemocratic Republic of Congo (DRC) uyabonwa ngokwenza okusha, ubuholi bakhe nomnikelo omangazayo kuya ekuphatheni nokwelapha izifo ezinjenge sifo segciwane i-Ebola. Usedlale indima ebucayi ekwenzeni ngcono imiphumela yezempilo eDemocratic Republic of Congo, isifunda sase-Afrika kanye nomphakathi wasemhlabeni. USolwazi Muyembe-Tamfum unguMqondisi Jikelele weDRC National Institute of Biomedical Research eKinshasa, uSolwazi ogcwele weMicrobiology eNyuvesi yaseKinshasa Medical School nomongameli wokwethula waseCongolese Academy of Science. USolwazi Muyembe-Tamfum wayeyingxenye yeqembu elathola ukuthi iZaire inegciwane lebhola ngesikhathi kuqoshwa okokuqala ukubheduka ngo-1976 eSifundazweni saseEquateur, Maphakathi neDRC.
translations.hypothesis: पुरस्कार זוले मना उपलब्ध हासिल उनी मा सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य। प्रोफेसर मुयेम्बे-ताम्फुम, देखि डेमोक्रेटिक रिपब्लिक अफ द कोन्गो (डीआरसी), ले आ कदरको लागि, साथै नवीनता, नेतृत्व, र रोगहरू जस्तै इबोला भाइरस रोगको व्यवस्थापन र उपचारमा महत्त्वपूर्ण योगदान। उनीले डेमोक्रेटिक रिपब्लिक अफ द कोन्गो, अफ्रिकन क्षेत्र, र विश्व समुदायमा स्वास्थ्य परिणाम सुधारमा महत्त्वपूर्ण भूमिका बजाली। प्रोफेसर मुयेम्बे-ताम्फुम, डीआरसीको राष्ट्रिय बायोमेडिकल अनुसन्धान संस्थाको निर्देशक, विश्वविद्यालय अफ किनशासा मेडिकल स्कूलमा माइक्रोबायोलोजिसको पूर्ण प्रोफेसर, र कोनगोस्के एकेडेमी अफ साइन्सको पहिलो अध्यक्ष। प्रोफेसर मुयेम्बे-ताम्फुम, ले 1976 मा, किनशासा, केन्द्रीय डीआरसी, च्याउरे प्रान्तमा पहिलो रेकर्डित प्रकोपमा, झायरे इबोलाभाइरसको खोजमा रहेको टोलीको सदस्य।

translations.source: He later helped design one of the first effective treatments against the virus and the deployment of experimental Ebola virus disease vaccines. He has worked tirelessly to build the Democratic Republic of the Congo’s scientific capacity, establishing multiple research facilities in the country, and has played key roles with WHO in fighting infectious diseases. Professor Piot from Belgium is being recognized for his extraordinary leadership in the response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic and other significant global health challenges. He has played a vital role in launching major global health initiatives such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund, and others that have improved health outcomes around the world. Under his leadership, UNAIDS became the chief advocate for worldwide action against AIDS, also spearheading UN reform by bringing together 10 different UN organizations.
translations.reference: Makhathaleni waseza ukwakha omunye wemishanguzo youqala esebenzayo ekulweni negciwane nokufakwa kokuhlolwa kwemigomo yesifo segciwane i-Ebola. Usesebenza ngokungakhathali ukwakha iDemocratic Republic of the Congo emandleni esayensi, ukwakha izindawo zokwenza ucwaningo oluningi ezweni, usedlale indima ebaluleke kakhulu neWHO ekulweni nezifo ezithelelanayo. USolwazi Piot waseBelgium uyabonakala ngobuholi obuhle kakhulu ekuphenduleni ngobhubhane lweHIV/AIDS kanye nezinye izingqinamba ezibalulekile zezempilo emhlabeni. Usedlale indima ebaluleke kakhulu ekwethulweni kwemizamo emikhulu yezempilo emhlabeni njenge-UNAIDS, iGlobal Fund, kanye neminye esiyenze ngcono imiphumela yezempilo emhlabeni wonke. Ngaphansi kobuholi bakhe, i-UNAIDS ibe ngabameli abakhulu emhlabeni wonke bokwenza okubhekene neAIDS kanye nokukhomba indlela ekwenzeni busha i-UN ngokuhlanganisa ndawonye izinhlangano ezahlukene ezingu-10 ze-UN.
translations.hypothesis: यद्यपि, पछि, उनले मद्दत गरे डिजाइन गर्न एउटा पहिलो प्रभावकारी उपचार विरुद्ध, र प्रयोगिक इबोला भाइरस रोग खोपहरूको कार्यान्वयन। उनले अथक परिश्रम गरेका छन्, सिर्प, वैज्ञानिक क्षमता, स्थापना, र विभिन्न अनुसन्धान केन्द्र, र WHO मा, लडिरहेको संक्रामक रोग। प्रोफेसर पियोट, बेलजियम, पहिचान गरिन्छ, उनको असाधारण नेतृत्व, प्रतिक्रियामा एचआईभी/एआईडीएस प्रकोप, र अन्य महत्त्वपूर्ण विश्वव्यापी स्वास्थ्य चुनौती। उनले महत्त्वपूर्ण विश्वव्यापी स्वास्थ्य पहविधिहरू, जस्तै UNAIDS, The Global Fund, र अन्य, कार्यान्वयनमा महत्त्वपूर्ण भूमिका खेलेका छन्, र यी पहविधिहरूले ज weltweit स्वास्थ्य परिणाम सुधार गरेका छन्। उनको नेतृत्वमा, UNAIDS बनेको एउटा मुख्य वकालतकर्ता, एआईडीएस विरुद्ध, र UN सुधार, 10 विभिन्न UN सङ्गठनहरूलाई भेला।

translations.source: Professor Piot also played a part in the earlier discovery of the Ebola virus, and the containment of the first known outbreak, participating in field epidemiological investigations. He has chaired a number of high-level initiatives, served in numerous high-level advisory roles, including on COVID-19, and has received multiple awards. Professor Piot is the Special Advisor to the EC President, a Professor of Global Health and former Director of the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. “These outstanding individuals embody lifelong dedication, relentless advocacy, a commitment to equity, and selfless service of humanity and people’s health,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “In a time of multiple emergencies, health crises, and conflict, we recognize that leadership in public health and science are more important than ever to advance global health.
translations.reference: USolwazi Piot ube yingxenye futhi yokusheshe kutholakale igciwane i-Ebola, nokulilawula ngesikhathi libheduka okokuqala lingaziwa, ukubamba iqhaza ekwenzeni ucwaningo ngezobhubhane. Usebe ngusihlalo wemizamo eminingi esezingeni eliphezulu,wasebenza emazingeni aphezulu amaningi njengomeluleki, okufaka nakuCOVID-19 usethole imiklomelo eminingi. USolwazi Piot unguMeluleki okhethekile kuMongameli wase-EC, uSolwazi weZempilo yoMhlaba owayenguMqondisi weLondon School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine. "Laba bantu abahlabahlosile banokuzinikela impilo yonke, ukumela, ukuzibophezela ekulinganeni, ukusebenzela abantu kanye nezempilo zabantu," kusho uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO, uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ngesikhathi sokuphuthumayo okuningi,izinkinga zezempilo nokuxabana,siyabubona ubuholi kwezempilo yomphakathi nesayensi kubaluleke kakhulu ukwenzangcono ezempilo emhlabeni.
translations.hypothesis: प्रोफेसर Piot ले पनि भूमिका महत्त्वपूर्ण, मा पहिलो पटक ईबोला भाइरसको खोज, र पहिलो ज्ञात प्रकोपको रोकथाम, मा भूमिका खेले, र क्षेत्रीय महामारीको खोजीमा। उनले धेरै उच्च-स्तरीय पहलहरू नेतृत्व गरेको, धेरै उच्च-स्तरीय सल्लाहकार भूमिकामा सेवा गरेको, सहित COVID-19, र धेरै पुरस्कारहरू प्राप्त गरेको। प्रोफेसर Piot ले, युरोपियन आयोगका अध्यक्षको विशेष सल्लाहकार, ग्लोबल हेल्थ प्रोफेसर, र लन्डन स्कूल अफ हाइजिन एन्ड ट्रोपिकल मेडिसिनका पूर्व निर्देशक। "यी उत्कृष्ट व्यक्ति, जीवन भरको समर्पण, अथक ललुप, समानताको प्रतिबद्धता, र मानवता र मानिसको स्वास्थ्य सेवाको निःस्वार्थ सेवा, " भन्यो WHO निर्देशक-सामान्य, डा. टेड्रोस अदहनोम गेब्रेयसस। "एकाधिक आपत्क, स्वास्थ्य संकट, र संघर्षको समयमा, हामीले हेसयस, सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य र विज्ञानमा नेतृत्व, विश्वव्यापी स्वास्थ्यको विकासको लागि अझै पनि महत्त्वपूर्ण।"

translations.source: Our awardees today personify that passion.” “Their combined stories and successes show how much the world has progressed in terms of collaboration at the global, regional and country levels to strengthen health emergency preparedness, response and resilience. These outstanding scientists will continue to inspire us every day,” he added The ceremony for the awards form part of the high-level opening session of the 76th World Health Assembly being held on Sunday 21 May 2023 in Geneva.
translations.reference: Abahlonishwa bethu namhlanje bakhombisa leyo ntshisekelo." "Izindaba zabo behlangene nokuphumelela kukhombisa ukuthi umhlaba usuqhubeke kangakanani ngokokubambisana emhlabeni,esifundeni nasemazingeni ezwe ukuqinisa ukuzilungiselela okuphuthumayo kwezempilo nokuphendula nokubekezela. Amasayensisi agqamile azoqhubeka ukugqugquzela zonke izinsuku," wengezile emcimbini wemiklomelo njengengxenye yeseshini yokuvula esezingeni eliphezulu lesi-76th World Health Assembly ozobanjwa ngesonto mhlaka-21 May 2023 eGeneva.
translations.hypothesis: "लाग्दारिता आज, तिनीहरूले तिनीहरूले।" "तिनीहरूको साझा कथा र सफलताले देखाउँछ कसले जति प्रगति हासिल गरेको छ, जसमा सहयोग, नเฉพาะ विश्व, तर क्षेत्रीय र राष्ट्रिय स्तरमा, ताकि स्वास्थ्यको आपत्कालीन तयारी, प्रतिक्रिया र लचीलापन बढाउन। यी उत्कृष्ट वैज्ञानिक, लगातार प्रेरणा गराउने," थप्यो। सो आयत, जुन 21, 2023, आइतवार, जेनेभामा, 76औं विश्व स्वास्थ्य सभाको उच्च-स्तरीय उद्घाटन, यस आयतको हिस्सा हो।

translations.source: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.”
translations.reference: I-WHO ihalalisela iBenin neMali ngokuqeda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi I-WHO isiqinisekise iBenin neMali ngokuqda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi,nokubenza abesihlanu nesithupha amazwe aseAfrika eWHO ukuthola umsebenzi omkhulu kangaka. Amazwe asethole ukugunyazwa okuvela kwiWHO ngokuqeda isifo samehlo yiGhana (ngoNhlangulana 2018) iGambia (ngoMbasa 2021) Togo (ngoNhlaba 2022 kanye neMalawi (ngoMandulo 2022). "WHO ihalalisela abasemagunyeni bezempilo baseBenin neMali kanye nohlelokuxhumana lwabo emhlabeni kanye nababambisani bendawo kulokhu asebekuzuzile," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Kulandela impumelelo yeBenin neMali isifo samehlo siyaqhubeka siba wubhubhane emazweni angu-23 eWHO African Region, kusiletha eduze kokuqedwa okuhlosiwe okuhleliwe kwesifo samehlo kubalazwe kulezofifo ezijwaelekile ezinganakiwe 2021-2030."
translations.hypothesis: WHO बधाई दि Benin र Mali ले ट्राहोमालाई हटाउनको निम्ति, WHO ले Benin र Mali लाई पुष्टि गरेको छ, र यसले उनीहरूलाई WHO ले African क्षेत्रमा यो महत्त्वपूर्ण उपलब्धि हासिल गरेको पाँचौ र छौ मुलुक बनाउँछ। मुलुकहरू जुन पहिले ट्राहोमा हटाउनको निम्ति WHO बाट मान्यता पाए, हुन् Ghana (जून 2018), Gambia (अप्रैल 2021), Togo (मे 2022) र Malawi (सितंबर 2022). "WHO बधाई दि Benin र Mali ले, तथा उहाँका स्थानीय र अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय साझेदारहरू, यस उपलब्धिको निम्ति", भन्यो Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO निर्देशक-सामान्य। "Benin र Mali ले सफलता हासिल गरेको पछिको, ट्राहोमा 23 मुलुकहरूमा WHO ले African क्षेत्रमा, यसले ट्राहोमा हटाउने लक्ष्यलाई प्राप्त गर्नको निम्ति, 2021-2030 मा स्थापित रोडमैपमा, हामी थप नजिक पुग्यौं।"

translations.source: Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.
translations.reference: Emhlabeni iBenin neMali bazojoyina amanye amazwe angu-15 asegunyazwe yiWHO ngokuqeda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi. Lawo mazwe yiCambodia, iChina, iGambia, iGhana, i-Islamic Republic of Iran, iLao Peoples Democratic Republic, iMalawi, iMexico, iMorocco, iMyanmar, iNepal, i-Oman, iSaudi Arabia, iTogo neVanuatu. Ukuqeda isifo samehlo usebenzisa isu leSAFE leWHO Womabili amazwe iBenin neMali benze isiphakamiso sesu lokuphepha leWHO ukuqeda isifo samehlo ngokwesekwa yiWHO nababambisani. Isu leSAFE liqukethe ukuhlinzwa ukuze welaphe isifo samehlo ngobunzia,ama-antibhayothikhi ukuqeda ukungcola,izinto zokuhlanza ubuso,ukwenzangcono indawo,ikakhulukazi ukwenza ngcono ukuthola amazi nokuthuthwa kwendle ukwehlisa ukudlulisela.
translations.hypothesis: वैविध्यपूर्ण रूपमा, बेनिन र माली सामेल भएमा 15 अन्य देशहरू, जुन WHO ले पुष्टि गरेको छ, र तिनीहरूले ट्राहोमालाई सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य समस्या हटाउन सफल भएका छन्। यी हुन्: कम्बोडिया, चीन, द गाम्बिया, घाना, इस्लामिक गणराज्य अफ इरान, लाओ लोकशाही जनप्रति, मलावी, मेक्सिको, मोरक्को, म्यानमार, नेपा, ओमान, सौदी अरेबिया, टोगो र वानuatu। ट्राहोमाको निवारण प्रयोग गर्दै WHO को SAFE रणनीति बेनिन र मालीले WHO र साझेदारहरहरूको समर्थनमा ट्राहोमा निवारणको लागि WHO द्वारा सिफारिस गरिएको SAFE रणनीति लागू गरेका छन्। SAFE रणनीति समावेश गर्दछ: सर्जिकल उपचारको लागि, देरको ट्राहोमा जटिलताहरू; संक्रमण हटाउनका लागि एन्टीबायोटिक्स; अनुहारको सफाई; र वातावरण सुधार, विशेष रूपमा पानी र स्वच्छताको पहुँच सुधार, संक्रमण घटाउन।

translations.source: Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD.
translations.reference: Ngomzamo wesifo samehlo wamazwe angaphandle,antibhayothikhi azithromycin xhase ngePfizer ekuqedweni kwezinhlelo zokwenza zesu leSAFE. I-Benin isihlanganise isifo samehlo ukuqedwa nokungenelela kulabo abaenza okubhekene nezinye izifo ezijwayelekile ezinganakiwe (amaNTD) ngaphansi kwesambulela seNationa Programme for Communicable Disesases. Isifo samehlo ngesesithathu kuNTD ukuqedwa eBenin emva kwedracunculiasis (ngo-2009) negambiese yabantu eAfrika trypanosomiasis (ngo-2021). Imali isicwaninge umthelela wesifo samehlo nocwaningo lokubheka nokukhipha izingenelelo ukuthola okuqondwe ukuqedwa,ngaphandle kwezingqinamba zokuphepha ezifundeni zasenyakatho yezwe nezinkinga zepolitiki yezenhlalakahle eminyakeni yakamuva nje. Isifo samehlo ngesokuqala seNTD ukuqedwa eMali ngakho manje esijoyina iqoqo lomhlaba lamazwe angu-47 aseqede okungenani esisodwa seNTD.
translations.hypothesis: यद्यपि पहि, Pfizer दान गर्छ, antibiotic azithromycin, मार्फत, कार्यक्रम जो SAFE रणनीति लागू, मार्फत। बेनिनले, trachoma निवार कार्यक्रम, ले अन्य उपेक्षित उष्णकटिबंधीय रोग (NTDs) विरुद्ध लागू गरिएका कार्यक्रमहरूसँग एकीकृत, राष्ट्रिय कार्यक्रमको अधीनमा। Trachoma, बेनिनमा, तेस्रो NTD हो, पछि dracunculiasis (2009 मा) र gambiense मानव अफ्रीकी ट्रिपानोसोमासिस (2021 मा)। मालीले, trachoma प्रभाव र निगरानी सर्वेक्षण गरेको, र निवार लक्ष्य हासिल गर्नका लागि हस्तक्षेप लागू गरेको, देशको उत्तर क्षेत्रमा सुरक्षा चुनौती र हालसालै सामाजिक-राजनीतिक उथलपुथलको बाबजूद। Trachoma, मालीमा, पहिलो NTD हो, जे अब, कम से कम एक NTD निवार गरिसकेका 47 देशहरूको विश्व समूहमा सामेल भयो।

translations.source: “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease.
translations.reference: "Lokhu ukuzuza kwezempilo yomphakathi okujabulisayo," kusho uDkt Ibrahima Soce Fall uMqondisi woHlelo lweNTD eMhlabeni lweWHO. "I-Benin nemali ikhombisileukuthi kuqine kanjani isifiso sezepolitiki,ukuhlangana kwemikhakha eyahlukene,ukubheka nokukhuluma komphakathi kungasebenza kusivumelwano sokuthola ukuqedwa kwesio." Ukuqhubeka okubalulekile sekwenzekile ekulweni okubhekene nesifo samehlo eminyakeni embalwa edlule. Abantu abaningi badinga kwelashwa nge antibhayothikhi ukuqeda isifo samehlo eWHO Afrikan Region abafika ezigidini ezingu-84 kusuka kuzigidi ezingu-189 ngo-2014 kuya kuzigidi ezingu-105 kusuka ngoNhlangulana 2022. Inothi eliya kumhleli: Isifo samehlo (Trachoma) sizohlala siyinkinga yezempilo yomphakathi emazweni angu-41 (kusuka ngoNhlangulana 2022) ngesibalo esilinganiselwe kuzigidi ezingu-125 zabantu abaphila ezindaweni ezidinga ukungenelela okubhekene nalesifo.
translations.hypothesis: “यी हुन् प्रभावशाली उपलब्धनाहरू, " भन्यो डा. इब्राहिमा सोसे फाल, निर्देशकको WHO ग्लोबल एनटीडी कार्यक्रम। "बेनिन र मालीले हेर्न सक्छन् कसरी शक्तिशाली राजनीतिक इच्छा, विभिन्न क्षेत्रको एकीकरण, निगरानी र समुदायको सहभागिता, सँगै काम गर्न सक्छन्, र रोगको निवारण हासिल गर्न।" महत्त्वपूर्ण प्रगति भइरहेको छ, लामो समययसयस, ल対रोग विरुद्ध। संख्या मानिसहरू, जे WHO अफ्रिकन क्षेत्रमा, जे आवश्यकताले ट्राहोमाको उपचार, घियो 84 मिलियन, देखि 189 मिलियन, 2014 मा, 105 मिलियन, जून 2022 सम्म। नोट: संपादनकर्ता: ट्राहोमा, 41 देशहरूमा (जून 2022 सम्म), एक सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य समस्या, र लगभग 125 मिलियन मानिसहरू, जे क्षेत्रहरू, जे आवश्यकताले रोग विरुद्ध हस्तक्षेप।

translations.source: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person.
translations.reference: Isifo samehlo sitholakala kakhulu ezindaweni ezingamakhaya futhi ezihluphekayo e-Afrika, Maphakathi naseNingizimu neAmerica, Asia naseWestern Pacific kanye neMiddle East. I-WHO African Region itheleleke yisifo samehlo kubantu abayizigidi ezingu 105 abahlala ezindaweni eziyingcuphe,ezimele u-48% womthwalo wesifo samehlo emhlabeni. Isifo samehlo isona esihamba phambili ekuthelelaneni esidala ukungaboni emhlabeni wonke. Isifo sidalwa ukuthelelana ngegciwane iChlamydia trachomatis. Ukuthelelana kudluliseka kumuntu kuye kumuntu ngeminwe engcolile, izinambuzane, izimpukane esezihlangabezane nokungcola okuphuma emahlweni noma ikhala lomuntu othelelekile.
translations.hypothesis: Trachoma मुख्य रूपमा भेट्टाउन सकिन्छ त, कतिपय धनी र ग्रामीण क्षेत्रहरू, जस्तै, अफ्रिका, मध्य र दक्षिण अमेरिका, एसिया, पश्चिमी प्यासिफिक र मध्य पूर्व। क्षेत्र अफ्रिका, अन्तर्गत WHO, विशेष रूपमा प्रभावित, जसमा 105 मिलियन व्यक्ति, जो जोखिमपूर्ण क्षेत्रहरूमा बसिरहेका छन्, जुन कूल ट्रॅकोमाको भारको 84% प्रतिनिधित्व गर्छ। ट्रॅकोमा विश्वव्यापी रूपमा, मुख्य कारणले, ज्यानको कारण। यो रोग, ट्रॅकोमाट्रिस जीवाणुको संक्रमणले निम्त्याइन्छ। संक्रमण, व्यक्ति देखि व्यक्ति, मार्फत, संक्रमित व्यक्तिको आँखा वा नाकबाट निस्सेको, दूषित औजार, र उज्याँले फैलन्छ।

translations.source: Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.reference: Izinkinga zengcephe yendawo yokudluliselwa kwesifo samehlo okufaka ukungahlanzeki,imizi egcwele ukungatholi kahle amanzi nokusebenzisa izindawo zokuzikhulula ezifanele. Ngo-1996, iWHO yethula iWHO Alliane yokuqedwa kwesifo samehlo emhlabeni ngonyaka ka-2020 (GET2020). Nabanye ababambisani kwi-Alliance, iWHO yeseka ukwenza kwezwe kweSu lokuPhepha nokuqinisa amandla ezwe ngokhlolwa kwezobhubhane, ukubheka, ukuhlola, ukubhekisa iphrojekthi nokuhlanganisa izinsizakusebenza ezizosiza ekuqedweni kwesifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi. Umgwaqo balazwe ogunyazwe nguMhlangano weZempilo eMhlabeni ngo-2020 ngesinqumo sawo 73(33) ibeka u-2030 njengosuku oluqondiwe olusha lokuqeda emhlabeni.
translations.hypothesis: कारकहरू जसले योगदान गराउँछन् मा ट्राहोमा फैलँदा समावेश छन्: खराब स्वच्छता, घने घर, अपर्याप्त पहुँच मा पानी, वा उचित सानिटरी सुविधा। मा 1996, WHO सुरु गरे WHO युनियनको उद्देश्य ट्राहोमाको ज Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020)। साथै, अन्य साझेदारहरूसँग, WHO देशको कार्यान्वयनको समर्थन, र जनस्वास्थ्य समस्याको उन्मूलनमा योगदान, epidemiological assessment, निगरानी, निगरानी, परियोजना मूल्याङ्कन, र स्रोत जुटाвање मार्फत, राष्ट्रिय क्षमतालाई बढावा। रोडमैप, जे 2020 मा विश्व स्वास्थ्य सभाले आफ्नो निर्णय 73(33) मार्फत, नयाँ लक्ष्य मिति 2030 निर्धारण गरेको छ।

translations.source: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families
translations.reference: Izigidi ezingu-152 izingane ezizalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi kushuminyaka elidlule Ezinganeni ezingu-10 ezizalwayo, eyodwa izalwa phambi kwesikhathi futhi emizuzwini engu-40 eyodwa kulezo ngane iyafa Amazinga okuzalwa kwezingane ngaphambi kwesikhathi awakashintshi kushuminyaka eledlule kunoma yisiphi isifunda emhlabeni Imithelela yengxabano,ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu,ingcuphe yokunyuka kweCOVID-19 kubantu besifazane nezingane yonke indawo Isu liyadingeka ngokushesha ukwenza ngcono ukuvikela ukuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi, okuhambisana nokunakekela okungcono ezinganeni ezikhahlamezekile kanye nemindeni yazo
translations.hypothesis: 152 मिलियन नसे जन्मिएका भन्दा मघा, मागील दस वर्षमा। पैकी प्रत्येक 10 नसे, 1 जन्मिएका हुन् – र प्रत्येक 40 सेकन्द्, 1 ती नसे मरे। दर जन्मिएका नसे मागील दस वर्षमा, कतै पनि संसारमा, परिवर्तन नगरिए। प्रभावहरू, संघर्ष, जलवायु परिवर्तन, र COVID-19, ले महिला र नसेलाई, कतै पनि, बढी जोखिम। तत्काल कार्य आवश्यक छ, ताकि नसेको मघा जन्म, सुधार, साथै, प्रभावित नसे र उनका परिवारलाई, राम्रो स्याहार।

translations.source: An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.reference: Isilinganiso sezigindi ezingu-13.4 izingane ezizalwe ngaphambi kwesikhathi nokungafika esigidini esisodwa semithelela yokufa, ngokombiko omusha okhishiwe yi-United Nations ama-ejensi kanye nababambisani namhlanje. Lo mzuliswano wokulingana wengane eyodwa kweziyishumi ezisheshe zizalwe (ngaphambi kwamasonto angu-37 okukhulelwa) emhlabeni wonke. Ukusheshe uzalwe: ishuminyaka lokwenza ekuzalweni ngaohambi kwesikhathi, kukhiqizwe Inhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni (WHO) ne United Nations Children Fund (UNICEF) ndawonye nePMNCH -- ubudlelwane obukhulu emhlabeni babantu besifazane, izingane, nentsha, kuveza ukuuthusa bokuthula kokuphuthumayo kwezokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi, okunesikhathi eside kunganakiwe esikalini sakho sobucayi okuveza ukuqhubeka ekwenzeningcono ezempilo zezingane nokuphila. Umbiko ukubuyekezwa kulinganisa okuphuma kuWHO ne-UNICEF, izilungiselele neLondon School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, ekusabalaleni kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi. Kukhona konke kutholakala ukuthi amazinga okuzalwa kwezingane ngaphambi kwesikhathi awakashintshi kunoma yisiphi isifunda emhlabeni kuleshuminyaka eledlule nezigidi ezingu-152 zezingane ezihluphekile ezisheshe zazalwa kusuka ngo-2010 kuya ku-2020.
translations.hypothesis: लगभग 13.4 मिलियन न Babies जन्म झाले ती अस्वसमय, согласно नयाँ रिपोर्ट जारी गरिएकोले संयुक्त राष्ट्रसंघका सङ्गठन र साझेदारहरू, आज। यसले लगभग 1 मिलियन न Babies, जो अस्वसमयका जटिलताहरूले मरे, समावेश। यो समतुल्य छ लगभग 1 मा 10 न Babies, जे अस्वसमय (37 हप्ताको गर्भको से) जन्म झाले। "अस्वसमय जन्म: दशको कार्य", उत्पादन गरिएकोले विश्व स्वास्थ्य सङ्गठन (WHO) र संयुक्त राष्ट्रसंघको बालFund (UNICEF), तथा PMNCH – विश्वको सबैभन्दा ठूलो गठबन्धन, जे महिला, बच्चाहरू, र किशोरहरू, "अस्वसमय जन्म" को "निसेलेको आपत्स्य" मा चेतावनी दिन्छ, जे लामो समयदेखि यसको पैमाना र गंभीरतामा मान्यता नदिएको छ, र जे बच्चाहरूको स्वास्थ्य र जीवनमा सुधारको प्रगति रोक्न। रिपोर्टमा, WHO र UNICEF ले तयार पारेका अद्यतन अनुमानहरू समावेश छन्, जे London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine ले तयार पारेकोले, अस्वसमय जन्मको प्रचलनमा। कुलमा, यसले फेला प Hallo छ जे अस्वसमय जन्मको दर विगत दशकोमा, विश्वका कुनै पनि क्षेत्रमा, परिवर्तन नगरिएको छ, जसमा 152 मिलियन कमजोर न Babies, जे अस्वसमय जन्म झाले, 2010 देखि 2020।

translations.source: Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive.
translations.reference: Ukuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi manje sekuyimbangela eholayo ekufeni kwezingane,ukubala okungaphezu kuka 1 wokungu 5 wakho konke ukufa okwenzeka ngaphambi kosuku lokuzalwa lweminyaka emihlanu. Abaphilayo kwabazalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi babhekana nemiphumela yezempilo impilo yabo yonke, ngokukhula kokujwayeleka kokukhubazeka kanye nokubambezeleka kokukhula. Izikhala zokuphila yisifunda, imali engenayo, ibala Ukwakhela emmbikweni wesikhonkwane esihlokweni sango-2012,lokhu kusheshe uzalwe, ishuminyaka, umbiko unikeza ukubhekwa okukhulu kokusabalala kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi nomthelela oqinile kwabesifazane, imiphakathi nezomnotho. Kujwayelekile lapho izingane zizalelwe khona kuzonquma ukuthi zizophila yini.
translations.hypothesis: जन्म भन्दा छिटो, अब मुख्य कारण ज्यान खोएका बालबालित, र यसले बालबालित ज्यान, जुन 5 बर्षको उमेर भन्दा पहिले, प्रतिनिधित्व गर्छ। बालबालित जो बाँचे, सक्छन् सामना गर्न दीर्घकालीन स्वास्थ्य परिणाम, जसमा जोखिम बढी छ। यो रिपोर्टले, "बालबालित जो छिटो जन्म",ले, 2012 मा प्रकाशित एक महत्त्वपूर्ण रिपोर्टमा आधारित, preterm जन्मको व्यापक अवलोकन प्रदान गर्दछ, र यसको महिला, परिवार, समाज र अर्थव्यवस्थामा गम्भीर प्रभाव। धेरैपटक, जहाँ बालबालित जन्म, निर्धारण गर्छ के उनी बाँचे।

translations.source: The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.
translations.reference: Umbiko uqaphela ukuthi uyedwa kuphela kwabangu-10 abantwana abasheshe bazalwa (<amasonto angu-28) abaphilayo emzaweni anemali encane engenayo,uma uqhathanisa kwangaphezu kwangu-9 ku-10 emazweni anemali ephezulu engenayo. Izikhala zokungalingani ezihlobene nebala, nobuzwe nemali engenayo nokthola ukunakekelwa okufanele kukhomba ukuphila kokusheshe uzalwe ukufa kanye nokukhubazeka ngisho nasemazweni anemali ephezulu engenayo. Southern Asia nesub-Saharan Afrika banamazinga aphezulu okusheshe kuzalwe kulezizizfunda futhi abhekene nengcuphe yokushona kwezingane ephezulu. Ndawonye, lezi zifunda ezimbili zibala ngaphezu kuka-65% wezingane ezisheshe zazalwa emhlabeni. Umbiko uphinde uveze umthelela wengxabano,ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu nokulimala kwendawo, COVID-19 nokunyuka kokubiza kwempilo okunyusa ingcuphe kubantu besifazane nezingane yonke indawo.
translations.hypothesis: रिपोर्टले उल्लेख छ, केवल 1 मा 10 अतिच vroeg जन्म भएका शिशु (28 ह Minggu) जीवित्नु, भन्दा तुलनात्मक रूपमा, 9 मा 10 उच्च आय भएका देशहरूमा जीवित्नु। असमानताहरू, जस्तै, वंश, जातीयता, आय, र उच्च-आय भएका देशहरूमा, איכותपूर्ण स्याही, निर्धारण गर्छन्, कि शिशु, मृत्यु, र अपंगता,। दक्षिण एसिया र सब-सहारन अफ्रिकामा, अतिच vroeg जन्मको दर, सबैभन्दा उच्च, र यी क्षेत्रहरूमा, अतिच vroeg जन्म भएका शिशुहरू, सबैभन्दा ठूलो जोखिमको सामना गर्छन्। दुई क्षेत्रहरू, संयुक्त रूपमा, विश्वव्यापी रूपमा, 65% भन्दा धेरै अतिच vroeg जन्महरू प्रतिनिधित्व गर्छन्। रिपोर्टले पनि उल्लेख गर्दछ, कि संघर्ष, जलवायु परिवर्तन, र वातावरणको क्षति, COVID-19, र बढिरहेको जीवन खर्च, सबै ठाउँमा महिला र शिशुहरूका लागि जोखिमहरू बढाउँछन्।

translations.source: For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism
translations.reference: Isibonelo, ukungcola komoya kulinganiselwa ukuthi kunomthelela wezigidi ezingu-6 zokusheshe kuzalwe unyaka ngamunye. Kungafika ku 1 kwabangu 10 izingane ezisheshe zazalwa ezizalwe emazweni abuthaka angu-10 akhahlamezwe izinkinga zabantu ngokokuhlaziya okusha embikweni. Ingcuphe yomama abakhulelwe, njengokukhulelwa kwamatshitshi kusondelene nokusheshe uzale. Lokhu kugqamisa isidingo sokuqinisekisa ukuthola ezempilo zokuzala nezocansi, okufaka ukusebenza kokuhlela umndeni, ukunakekelwa okusezingeni eliphezulu kwabakhulelwe nangesikhathi sokuzala. Ajenda yokwenza: utshalomali oluningi ezweni nokushoshova okuholwa ngabazali
translations.hypothesis: उदाहरणको, प्रदूषणEstimated योगदानमा 6 मिलियन नसबली प्रत्येक वर्ष। लगभग 10 मध्ये 1 नसबली नसबली जन्म भइन्छ, क अनुसार नया विश्लेषण, मा रिपोर्ट। मा, माते स्वास्थ्य जोखिम, जस्तै, किशोर गर्भावस्था र प्री-एक्ल्याम्पसिया, निकट सम्बद्धता छ साथ नसबली नसबली। यसले, उच्च-गुणवत्ता सेवा, सहित, परिवार नियोजन, र गर्भावस्था र जन्मको समयमा, आवश्यक छ। कार्यको एजेंडा: थप देशको लगानी र अभिभावक-नेतृत्व

translations.source: The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care.
translations.reference: Ishuminyaka eledlulesibone ukukhula kwezishoshovu emphakathini ekuvinjweni kokusheshe kuzalwe kwezingane, okuholwa uhlelokuxhumana lwabazali, izifundiswa zempilo, izifundiswa umphakathi nabanye. Ezweni lonke amaqoqo emindeni ekhahlamezekile ngokusheshe kuzalwe kwengane alokhu ephambili ekumeleni ukuthola ukunakekelwa okungcono nokushinthwa kwenqubomgomo nokweseka imindeni. Okuholela kuInternational Maternal Newborn Helath Conference, eKapa eNingizimu Afrika kusuka ngo-8-11 kuNhlaba, iWHO, i-UNICEF, i-UNFPA nePMNCH bamemezela ukulandela izenzo ukwenza ngcono ukunakekela kwabesifazane nabazelwe ubukhulu bezingcuphe okuvela kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi. Ukunyuswa kotshalomali:ukuhlanganisa izinsizakusebenza zamazwe angaphandle nezendawo ukusiza ezempilo zomama nezingane ezizelwe, ukuqinisekisa ukunakekelwa okuphezulu kuyanikezwa uma noma nalapho kudingeka khona. Ukunyuswa kokwenza: Ukuhlangabezana nokuqondwe ngamazwe kokuqhubeka ngokwenza okusungulwe yinqubomgomo yezwe kokunakekelwa komama nezingane ezizelwe.
translations.hypothesis: दशकयय पनि, गतिविधि समुदायले, विशेषतः प्रसूति र मृत शिशु रोकथाम,ले पनि विकसित भयो, र यसले अभिभावक, स्वास्थ्य विशेषज्ञ, शैक्षिक संस्था, नागरिक समाज, र अन्य संस्थाहरूको सहयोग प्राप्त गरेको छ। विश्वव्यापी रूपमा, समूहहरूले प्रसूतिमा प्रभावित परिवारहरूलाई, समर्थन गर्न, र राम्रो सेवा र नीति परिवर्तनको लागि वकालत गरेका छन्। अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय मातृ-बाल स्वास्थ्य सम्मेलन, जो केप टाउन, दक्षिण अफ्रिका मा, 8-11 मे मा, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA, र PMNCH, महिला र नवजात शिशुहरूको हेरफेर सुधार गर्न र प्रसूतिमा जोखिमहरू कम गर्न, निम्न कार्यहरूलाई आह्वान गरेका छन्: बढी लगानी: मातृ र नवजात शिशु स्वास्थ्यलाई अनुकूल बनाउन, अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय र स्थानीय स्रोतहरूलाई जुटाउन, र जहाँ आवश्यक छ, उच्च-गुणवत्ता सेवा प्रदान सुनिश्चित गर्न। तेगडा कार्यान्वयन: मातृ र नवजात शिशु सेवाको लागि स्थापित राष्ट्रिय नीतिको कार्यान्वयन मार्फत, देशका लक्ष्य हासिल गर्न।

translations.source: Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon.
translations.reference: Ukuhlanganisa kuyona yonke imikhakha: Ukugqugquzela ezemfundo ngempilo, ukweseka utshalomali lwezomnotho oluhlakaniphile, nokubambisana kwezimali kuyona yonke imikhakha, ukuqinisa ukuhambisana kwesimo sezulu nokuphendula kuyona yonke impilo, ukwenza ngcono ukuxhumana nokubekezela ezinhlelweni eziphuthumayo. Okwenziwa abendawo okusha: ukutshala endaweni okuholwa okusha nocwaningo lokweseka intuthuko yokunakekelwa okuhle nokuthola ngokulingana. Okucashuniwe: uSolwazi Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine Omunye wabaholayoUsheshe wazalwa-ishuminyaka lwesenzo ekushesheni uzalwe (2023) noKusheshe uzalwe (2012) "Lombiko omusha ukhombisa ukuthi ukubiza kokungenzi kulelishuminyaka eledlule kwakuyizigidi ezingu-152 ezisheshe zazalwa.
translations.hypothesis: एकाीकरण तर्फे: प्रोत्साहनको माध्यमबाट शिक्षा; बुद्धिमानी आर्थिक लगानी, साथै विभिन्न क्षेत्रहरूमा सह-वित्तपोषण; जीवनचक्रभर जलवायु अनुकूलन प्रतिक्रियाहरूलाई मement; र आपत्कालीन प्रणालीको समन्वय र लचीलापन। स्थानीय-आधारित नवोन्मेष: स्थानीय नेतृत्वको नवोन्मेष र अनुसन्धानमा लगानी, ताकि सेवाको איכות र पहुँचमा सुधार। उदहारण: प्रा. जोय लौन, लन्डन स्कूल अफ हाइजिन & ट्रोपिकल मेडिसिन, सह-अध्यक्ष, "जन्म लगतै": दशको कार्य (2023) र "जन्म लगतै" (2012) "यस नयाँ रिपोर्टले देखाउँछ कि, पछिल्ला दशको निष्क्रियताको लागत थियो 152 मिलियन, जन्म लगतै।

translations.source: While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country"
translations.reference: Ngesikhathi kwezinye izifunda bekhahlamezeke kakhulu ukusheshe uzale kusabisa ukuqhubeka kwezempilo kuwona wonke amazwe. Utahlomali olukhulu ekunakekelweni kwabazelwe ababuthaka kungasindisa izigidi zemindeni ekuphukeni kwenhliziyo. Umsebenzi omuningi uyadingeka ukugwema ukusheshe kuzalwe, okuzophinde kwenze ngcono ukuqhubeka kokwehlisa ukufa komama nezingane ezisheshe zazalwa. Ndawonye lokhu okubili kokuvikela ukusheshe kuzalwe nokunakekelwa kuzokhiqiza abantu abanempilo nemiphakathi ukuletha kwezomnotho kanye nokuthuthukiswa kwenhlalakahle. Isizukulwane sethu esizayo sincike kuthina kukhona konke esikwenzayo manje-utshalomali ngeke lube luncane kodwa izimbuyiselo kulolutshalomali luzoba lukhulu kakhulu wonke amazwe"
translations.hypothesis: यद्यपि केही क्षेत्र प्रभावित, जन्म भन्दा लगत, स्वास्थ्य प्रगति मा खतरा छ, रयको। थप लगानी मा कमजोर नवजात शिशुको स्याही, हजारौ परिवारलाई दु:खबाट जोगाउन सक्छ। थप काम पनि आवश्यक छ, ताकि जन्म भन्दा लगत, जेले पनि, स्टिलबर्थ र मातृ मृत्यु दर घटाउन मद्दत गर्ने। दुईवटा यो मार्ग - जन्म भन्दा लगतको रोकथाम र स्याही, स्वस्थ व्यक्ति र समाज निर्माणमा योगदान गर्ने। हाम्रो भविष्य निर्भर छ, र हामी सबै तत्काल कार्य गरौ - लगानीले सानो नहुन सक्छ, तर यो लगानी प्रत्येक देशको लागि महत्त्वपूर्ण फन्दा।

translations.source: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) “There is now no excuse for silence. We now have in our hands the devices and the knowledge to change outcomes for our most vulnerable newborns. I am proud to see my government now investing and committed to implementation across even rural areas. The next decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere.”
translations.reference: uDkt Nahya Salim,waseTanzania: Ukusheshe uzalwe omunye wababhali nezwe eliholwa ngeNewborn Essentials Solutions and Technology 360 (NEST360) "Asisekho manje isizathu sokuthula. Manje sinamadivayisi ezandleni zethu kanye nolwazi lukushintsha imiphumela yezingane zethu ezihluphekile ezizelwe. Ngiyaziqhenya ukubona uhulumeni wethu manje etshala futhi ezibophezela ukwenza yonke indawo ngisho nasemakhaya. Ishuminyaka elizayo lingaba futhi kumele lihluke kulabo ababhekene zokusheshe bazale lana nakuyona yonke indawo."
translations.hypothesis: डा Nahya Salim, मडागास्कर: सह-लेखक र्या "जन्म समयमै", र्या राष्ट्रिय नेता र्या "Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360)" "अब אין कारण न मौन। अब, हामी छौस, उपकरण र ज्ञान, ताकि हामीสามารถ फेरबदल गराउन, र हाम्रा धेरै कमजोर नवजात शिशुहरू। म खुसी छु कि सरकारमा अब लगानी गरिएको छ र यसले कार्यान्वयनको प्रतिबद्धता छ, यहाँ र सबैभन्दा ग्रामीण क्षेत्रहरूमा। अर्को दशक, र यसले निश्चित रूपमा फरक हुनुपर्छ, र यो यहाँ र सबैत्रो, प्रिमिट जन्मको सामना गरिरहेका व्यक्तिहरूका लागि।"

translations.source: Dr Anshu Banerjee, Director for Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing at WHO “Ensuring quality care for these tiniest, most vulnerable babies and their families is absolutely imperative for improving child health and survival. Progress is also needed to help prevent preterm births -- this means every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks.” Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF “After every preterm death is a trail of loss and heartbreak.
translations.reference: UDkt Anshu Banerjee, uMqondisi wabakhulelwe, abazelwe, izingane nentsha kwezempilo kanye nokuguga eWHO "Ukuqinisekisa ukunakekelwa okufanele kwabancane, abantekenteke nemindeni yabo kufanele impela nokwenzangcono ezempilo nokuphila komntwana. Inqubekela phambili iyadingeka futhi ukusiza ukuvikela ukusheshe bazale-lokhu kusho ukuthi wonke umuntu wesifazane kumele akwazi ukuthola ezempilo ezifanele ngaphambi nangesikhathi sokukhulelwa ukuze kuhlonzwe futhi kuphathwe izingcuphe." USteven Lauwerier, uMqondisi weZempilo (a.i.), e-UNICEF "Ngemva kokufa kokusheshe uzalwe njalo ukulandelana kokulahlekelwa nokuphuka kwenhliziyo.
translations.hypothesis: डा. अन्शु बानर्जी, निर्देशक माम, नवजात, बालबालित र वृद्ध स्वास्थ्य र उम्र, कय "सुनिश्चित पार्ने कि यी सान, र कमजोर शिशुहरू र उनका परिवारहरू प्राप्त उच्च-गुणवत्ता सेवा, अनिवार्य छ। यसले बालबालितको स्वास्थ्य र बाँचेको सुधार। साथै, आवश्यक छ כדי रोकी, जे हो भने, यो अर्थ्याले, प्रत्येक महिलाले गर्भधारण अगाडि र केबल, उच्च-गुणवत्ता स्वास्थ्य सेवाहरू प्रयोग गर्न सक्षम हुनुपर्छ, ताकि जोखिमहरू पहिचान र व्यवस्थापन गर्न सकू।" स्टीवन लाउवेरिअर, निर्देशक स्वास्थ्य (अ.ि.), युएनसेफ "यस पछि, प्रत्येक नवजात शिशुको मृत्यु, दु:ख र हृदयविदार।

translations.source: Despite the many advances the world has made in the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of small babies born too soon or averting the risk of their death. The toll is devasting. It’s time we improve access to care for pregnant mothers and preterm infants and ensure every child gets a healthy start and thrives in life.” Helga Fogstad, Executive Director, PMNCH “Born too soon underlines why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth – the world’s largest cause of death of children under the age of five.
translations.reference: Ngaphandle kobungcono okuningi emhlabeni esikwenzile eshumininyaka eledlule, asikenzi nqubekela phambili ekwehliseni inamba yezingane ezincane ezisheshe zizalwe noma sivimbe ingcuphe ekuzalweni kwabo. Loluhambo luyakhubaza. Yisikhathi sokwenza ngcono ukuthola ukunakekelwa komama abakhulelwe kanye nezingane ezizalwe kungashayi isikhathi nokuqinisekisa ukuthi yonke ingane ithola ukuqala kwezempilo nokuphokophela empilweni." UHelga Fogstad, uMqondisi Omkhulu, wePMNCH Ukusheshe uzalwe kusho incazelo yokuthi kumele sinyuse utshalomali nesibophezelo kwabasheshe bazalwa - imbangela enkulu emhlabeni yokufa kwezingane ezingaphansi kweminyaka emihlanu.
translations.hypothesis: यद्यपि दुन्यानु, मागील दस वर्ष, हासिल गरेको धेरै प्रगति, हामीले अझै पनि नबु, कसले धेरै छ, वा जोखिम उनको मृत्यु, कम गर्न सकेनौ। यो स्थिति निक बेर. अब, हामीले गर्भवती आमाहरू र नवजात शिशुहरूलाई हेस, र सुनिश्चित गनु पर्छ, हरेक बच्चाले स्वस्थ जीवन सुरु र विकसित हुन।" हेलगा फोगस्टेड, कार्यकारी निर्देशक, पीएमएनसीएच "धेरै छ, यसले स्पष्ट बनाउँछ, के हामीले नवजात शिशु जन्मको लगानी र उत्तरदायित्व बढावा, - जुन दुन्यानु, पाँच वर्ष जुनी कम आयुमा, बच्चाहरूको मृत्युको मुख्य कारण हो।

translations.source: Progress is flatlining for maternal and newborn health, as well as the prevention of stillbirths, and is now pushed back further through the devastating combination of COVID-19, climate change, expanding conflicts and rising living costs. “By working together in partnership – governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can sound the alarm about this “silent emergency”, and bring preterm prevention and care efforts to the forefront of national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, societies and economies everywhere. ”Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, and founders of Jalen’s Gift Foundation “Our foundation was founded after the loss of our son, Jalen Foster, who was a preterm stillbirth at 27 weeks due to prenatal onset of a bacteria known as group B streptococcus. Losing our son was devastating, and there was no support in the community and that’s when we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a baby.
translations.reference: Ukuqhubeka kokulinganisa kumama nezempilo zomntwana ozelwe, kanye nokuvikelwa kokuzalwa kwabantwana kungakashayi isikhathi, manje sekubekwe emuva ngokuhlanganiswa okukhathazayo kweCOVID-19, ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu, ukwandisa izingxabano nokukhulisa izindleko zokuphila. Ngokusebenza ndawonye ngokubambisana - uhulumeni, abaxhasi, umkhakha ozimele, umphakathi, abazali, izifundiswa zempilo - singaveza inkinga ngokuthula kokuphuthuma nokuletha ukuvikelwa kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi nemizamo yokunakelewa phambili kwezempilo yezwe nemizamo yokuthuthukisa, ukwakha amandla omuntu ngokweseka imindeni, imiphakathi nezomnotho yonke indawo. "UGabriela noJerome Foster, e-Las Vegas, e-United States: abazali baJalen Foster, abasunguli beJalen Gift Foundation "Indawo yethu yasungulwa ngemva kokushona komfana wethu, uJalen Foster, owasheshe wazalwa enamasonto angu-27 ngenxa yokusheshe ngisikwe ngegciwane elingaziwa njengeqoqo B streptococcus. Ukulahlekelwa yindodana yethu kwasifakela ingcindezi futhi kwakungekho ukwesekwa emphakathini nokuthi ngesikhathi sinquma ukunikezela ngendawo ukududuza nokunika ithemba emindenini elahlekelwe ngumntwana.
translations.hypothesis: प्रगतिमाथ, स्वास्थ्य, रोकथाम, र मृत नवजात शिशु, छ, र अब यसले COVID-19, जलवायु परिवर्तन, विस्तारित संघर्ष, र बढिर ग्याँतको विनाशकारी संयोजनको कारण थप कमजोर भइरहेको छ। "हामी, सरकार, दाता, खाजगी क्षेत्र, नागरिक समाज, अभिभावक, र स्वास्थ्य विशेषज्ञ, एकअर्का साथ काम गरेर, "यस" मौन आपत्स्य" बारे चेतावनी दिन सक्छौ, र प्रिमेट रोकथाम र हेरचो प्रयासहरूलाई राष्ट्रिय स्वास्थ्य र विकास प्रयासहरू मा प्राथमिकता दिन सक्छौ, परिवार, समाज र अर्थव्यवस्थाहरूलाई समर्थन गरेर मानव स्रोत विकास गर्न सक्छौ।" गेब्रिएला र जेरोम फोजर, लास वेगास, संयुक्त राज्य अमेरिका: जेलेन फोजरका अभिभावक, र जेलेनस गिफ्ट फाउन्डेसनका संस्थापक "हामका फाउन्डेसन, पछि, हाम्रो छोरा, जेलेन फोजर, 27 हप्ताको प्रिमेट मृत शिशु, जे समूह बी स्ट्रेप्टोकोकस नामक जीवाणुको कारण, स्थापना भयो। हामका छोरा खोसिएको विनाशकारी थियो, र समुदायमा कुनै समर्थन थिएन, र त्यसबेला हामीले निर्णय गरे कि हामीले उनिहरूका लागि आराम र आशाको स्थान प्रदान गरौ, जेले बच्चा खोसिएको छ।

translations.source: We didn’t want other families to feel like we did – alone. So, we started Jalen’s Gift Foundation to help other families going through a loss, to provide multiple free services, such as photograph, grief support and help towards memorial services.” ***Download the Born too soon media resources
translations.reference: Besingafuni eminye imindeni izezwe njengba sazizwa - uwedwa. Ngakho, siqale iJalen's Gift Foundation ukusiza imindeni elahlekelwe, ukubanikeza usizo lwamahhala oluningi, njengezithombe, ukwesekwa kokuzila nokubasiza ezinhlelweni zesikhumbuzo." ***Landa usheshe wazalwa kuzinsizakusebenza zezindaba
translations.hypothesis: हाथिर चाहँ अन्य परिवार महसूस गनु, जस्तो कि हामी – एक्लो। त्यसैले, हामीले "Jalen's Gift Foundation" स्थापना, ताकि मद्दत गर्न सकू अन्य परिवारहरू जसले हानि भोगे, र विभिन्न निःशुल्क सेवाहरू प्रदान गर्न, जस्तै: तस्बिर, भावनात्मक समर्थन, र अनुस्मारक सेवाहरू।

translations.source: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict.
translations.reference: I-UEA neWHO ihambise iziphakamisi moya zamasaplayi ezokwelapha ezibucayi eSudan United Arab Emirates (UAE) neNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni (WHO) bahambise amathoni angu-30 amasaplayi ezokwelapha okuphuthumayo eSudan namhlanje. Ibhanoyi eliphethe amasaplayi yokwelapha abalimele, ukuhlinza okuphuthumayokanye nezidakamizwa eziphuthumayo lifike isikhumulweni samabhanoyi iPort Sudan ekuseni kakhulu. Ukuhanjswa kwenani elingu US$ 444 000, eyokuqala iWHO ekwazile ukuyihambisa ngezomoya iya eSudan kusukela kube nokubheduka kwengxabano. I-WHO ihambise amasaplayi ezindaweni zempilo ngaphambi kokunyuka kwengxabano.
translations.hypothesis: युएई र विश्व स्वास्थ्य संगठनले, अत्यावश्यक चिकित्सा सामग्रीहरूलाई सुडान पुगराए। संयुक्त अरब एमिरात (युएई) र विश्व स्वास्थ्य संगठन आज सुडानलाई 30 टन अत्यावश्यक चिकित्सा सामग्री पुगराए। एउटा विमान, मार्ने उपचार, அவ Emergency शल्यक्रिया र आवश्यक औषधिको सामग्री ले, आज बिचतमा पोर्ट सुडान एयरोपोर्टमा पुग्यो। यो शिपमेन्ट, जो US$ 444,000 मूल्यको छ, पहिलो हो जेले विश्व स्वास्थ्य संगठनले सुडानलाई उडानमार्फत पुगराए,यस पछि संघर्ष सुरु भयो। विश्व स्वास्थ्य संगठनले, संघर्ष तीव्र हुनु अघि, स्वास्थ्य सेवा केन्द्रहरूलाई सामग्रीहरू वितरण गरेको।

translations.source: These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.reference: Babekhathele ngemva kwezinsuku ezimbalwa uma ubheka inamba yabalimele. Ukukhishwa kwendizamshini kuza njengengxenye ye-UAE imizamo yokuqhubeka nokukhulula ekwesekeni abantu baseSudanese nokuveza ubuhlobo obunzulu neSudan, ikakhulukazi ngokwesimo samanje esibhekene nezwe. Ukukhululwa kuphinde kube ukululwa kobuntu be-UAE umbono nesikalo sobudlelwano namanye amazwe ancike kubantu nouzibophezela kokusiza abanye ngezikhathi zezinkinga nezimo eziphuthumayo. UDkt Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Unqongqoshe wezwe leInternational Cooperation, uthe i-UAE izoqhubeka nokusebenzisana neNhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni ukuqinisekisa ukubekwa okuyimpumelelo kokusebenza kwelojistikhi ebucayi nokuthumela ezokwelapha ngokuphuthuma nosizo lokudla ukusiza ukubhekana nenkinga yamanje eSudan. Nokuhambisana nesimemezelo kuzona zonke izinngxenye zengxabano yamanje ukuyeka manje unya nokuqhuba isimo esidala ukuhlupheka okungashiwo kubantu baseSudan, i-UAE isheshile ekuzibophezelei kwayo ukunikeza usizo nokusiza mazwe ngesikhathi beludinga.
translations.hypothesis: यी स्रोत थोर भए, र selepas केही दिन, रयले। पठाउन विमान आउँछ, र यसले UAE ले लगातार प्रयास गर्न, र Sudanese मानिसलाई मद्दत गर्न, र यसले Sudan ले, विशेष रूपमा, हालको परिस्थितिमा, गहिरो सम्बन्ध। यस मद्दतले UAE ले मानवीय दृष्टिकोण, र यसले अन्य देशहरूसँग सम्बन्ध, र यसले संकट र அவिल परिस्थितिमा, अरुलाई मद्दत गर्न, प्रतिबद्धता। डा. रीम बिन एब्राहिम अल हशमी, राज्य मन्त्री,ले बताए, "UAE, विश्व स्वास्थ्य सङ्गठनसँग, Sudanese क्रान्तिको समाधान गर्न, अत्यावश्यक चिकित्सा र भोजन मद्दत, सफलतापूर्वक पठाउन, काम गरिरहेको छ। यसैको अतिरिक्त, सबै पक्षहरूले तत्काल युद्धविराम गर्न र Sudanese मानिसलाई जोगाउन, UAE, मद्दत र सहायता प्रदान गर्न प्रतिबद्ध छ।

translations.source: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need.
translations.reference: I-UAE igxile kakhulu ekunikezeni amaqoqo abuthaka kakhulu akhahlamezekile kulesisimo saseSudan, ikakhulukazi abagulayo, izingane,nabadala nabesifazane abasengcupheni yokuqhubeka kwengxabano - futhi lamabhanoyi akhululayo azobhekana ngqo nezikhala eziphuthumayo kwezokulapha kanye nokuhlinzekwa ngokudla. I-UAE igcine ngokujula ubuntu kusho ukuthi isiqinisekisile iyaqhubeka nokuxhumana nokumhlaba okukhulu nokuzinikela kokuqinisa ukuthula, ukuphepha nozinzo, ayi esizindeni kuphela kodwa emhlabeni wonke ngesikhathi nababambisani nomphakathi wamazwe angaphandle, izwe liyaqhubeka nokusiza iSudanese ezikhathini ezinzima. Amasaplayi ezempiloafaka ukuthuka okwanele, amasaplayi okuhlinza okuphuthumayo nemithi ebalulekile ukuthi ifike manje kubantu abangu-165 000 abadinga kakhulu usizo lobuntu. Izindawo zezempilo ezweni lonke zibika ukuthi seziphelelwe masaplayi ayisisekelo sezempilo nokuthi izitolo zokwelapha zezwe akusafinyeleki kuzona ngenxa yezokuphepha ezweni. Kokunye ukwesekwa, iWHO izobeka amalojistikhi amabili okuphuthumayo ukuhamba ngebhanoyi ukuqinisekisa lamasaplayi asehanjiswa ezindaweni zempilo ezinkulu ezingu-13 ukweseka abasebenzi bezempilo nokwengeza ukunakekela kulabo abakudingayo.
translations.hypothesis: युएई विशेष रूपमा केन्द्रित छ, र यसले लक्षित समूह, विशेष रूपमा बिमार, बच्चाहरू, वृद्ध, र महिलाहरू, जे जोखिममा छन् – र यस चलिरहेको चिंतातको संघर्ष, र यी राहत उडानहरूले प्रत्यक्ष रूपमा चिकित्सा र भोजन प्रदानमा कति आवश्यकताहरू छन्, यसलाई समाधान गर्ने। युएईको गहिरो मानवीय मूल्यहरूले सुनिश्चित गरेको छ, र यसले दुनिल, सुरक्षा र स्थिरतालाई मement, न केवल क्षेत्रीय, तर विश्वव्यापी रूपमा, यसले आफ्नो साझेदारहरू र अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय समुदायसँगै, देशले क्राइसिसको समयमा सुडानवासीहरूलाई मement। स्वास्थ्य सामग्री समावेश पर्याप्त मात्रा, அவिलन्त शल्य चिकित्सा सामग्री, र आवश्यक औषधीहरू, जे तत्काल 165,000 व्यक्तिहरूलाई मement, जे मानवीय मement आवश्यक छ। देशभरि, स्वास्थ्य प्रतिष्ठानहरूले रिपोर्ट गरेका छन्, जे आधारभूत स्वास्थ्य सामग्री समाप्त भएका छन्, र सुरक्षाको अवस्थाले, राष्ट्रिय चिकित्सा भण्डारहरू अब उपलब्ध छैनन्। अन्य मement, विश्व स्वास्थ्य संगठन (WHO) दुई அவिलन्त रसद विशेषज्ञहरू, जे कार्गोसहित यात्रा गर्ने, जे सुनिश्चित गर्ने, जे यी सामग्रीहरू तत्काल 13 मुख्य स्वास्थ्य प्रतिष्ठानहरूमा वितरण गरिन, जे स्वास्थ्य работकहरूलाई मement र जे आवश्यकता छ, जे मement।

translations.source: WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.reference: WHO inamathoni amakhulu angu-30 amasaplayi amalalwveva nezifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo njengehayihayi noshukela, izimo ezingabulala uma zishiywa zingalashiwe. Lokhu nezikhwama zegazi ezingu-23 000 sezilungisiwe nelojistikhi yomhlaba yeWHO uhabhu kuInternational Humanitarian City neWHO njengamanje ibheka zonke izindlela zokuletha lamasaplayi eSudan ngokushesha ekubambisaneni noNgqongqoshe weZempilo eSudan. UDkt Michael Ryan, uMqondisi omkhulu wohlelo lweziphuthumayo zempilo weWHO, uthe Iwho Global Logistic Hub e-UAE isibe okubalulekile kokuphindeka kwempendulo yesaplayi esigamekweni ezibucayi emhlabeni. Ukweseka amazwe angaphezu kuka-140 kuzona zonke izindawo zezifunda zweWHO, ihabhu inikezela ngempilo yonke yeMember States kusipiliyoni sokuphuthumayo kwezempilo okuvelayo ekubhedukeni kwezifo, izinhlekelele njengezikhukhula nokuzamazama komhlaba, nomthelela wengxabano ezinhlelweni zempilo. Ukwesekwa okunikeziwe yi-UAE ivumele iWHO ukuthi ibe phambili ekuphenduleni kweziphuthumayo zempilo-ukuhambisa usizo longoti bezokwelapha kulabo abadingayo lapho kudingeka khona.
translations.hypothesis: WHO छ, 30 टन थोर रसद सामग्री, र मलेरिया र गैर-संक्रामक रोगहरू जस्तै मधुमेह र उच्च रक्तचाप, जुन यदि उपचार नगरि, हानिकारक हुन सक्छन्। यी र लगभग 23,000 रक्त थैले, तयारी भइरहेका छन्, र WHO, अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय मानवीय शहरमा, WHO ग्लोबल логистика केन्द्र, र WHO, हालै, Sudanese Ministry of Health संग सहकार्य गर्दै, यी रसद सामग्रीहरूलाई सकेसम्म चाँडो सुडान पुग्या لپاره, सबै सम्भावनाहरू खोजीय. डा. माइकल रायन, WHO Health Emergencies Programme, कार्यकारी निर्देशक, भन्छन्, "WHO ग्लोबल логистика केन्द्र, UAE मा, छ, र यसले ज weltweit, तीव्र रसद प्रतिक्रियाको केन्द्र बनेको छ. यो केन्द्र, 6 WHO भौगोलिक क्षेत्रहरूमा, 140 भन्दा बढी देशहरूलाई, समर्थन प्रदान गर्दछ, र सदस्य राज्यहरूलाई, रोगहरूको प्रकोप, जस्तै बाढी र भूकम्प, र संघर्षले स्वास्थ्य प्रणालीमा उत्पन्न हुने, स्वास्थ्य आपत्कालीन अवस्थामा, जीवन रेखा प्रदान गर्दछ. UAE द्वारा प्रदान गरिएको समर्थन, WHO लाई, यी स्वास्थ्य आपत्कालीन प्रतिक्रियाहरूमा, अग्रिम रहने, र जहाँ र कहिले आवश्यक छ, उनहरूलाई विशेषज्ञ चिकित्सा मद्दत प्रदान गर्न, सक्षम बनाउँछ.

translations.source: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies.
translations.reference: Njenge ngxenye yokuqinisa ezokwakha umhlaba kwezokulungiselela eziphuthumayo zempilo, ukuphendula nokubekezela Iuae, iWHO nokubambisana bayasebenzisana ukuhambisa amasaplayi ukufinyelela kwababuthaka kakhulu emhlabeni esikhathini sabo abadinga usizo ngayo. Ngengxenye yakhe, uDkt Ahmed Al-Mandhari, uMqondisi wesifunda seWHO wase-Eastern Mediterranean, uthe iNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni izoqhubeka nokuxhumanisa nabasemagunyeni bezempilo eSudan ukuletha amasaplayi kulabo ababuthaka kakhulu emhlabeni esikhathi sokudinga usizo kakhulu. Ngenxa yokuxabana, izibhedlela, amakhemisi nezindawo zokunakekela ezempilo yokuqala seziphelelwe okubalulekile, okuyimithi yokusindisa izimpilo. Ukufika kwamathani amakhulu angeziwe awu-30 okukhathazeka namasaplayi ezokuhlinza okuphuthumayo kanye nemithi ebalulekile ukukwazi iWHO izokweseka izindawo zempilo ezinkulu ezingu-13 nokwenza kabusha kwezempilo umsebenzi wokunakekela kulabo abadingayo. Ilojistikhi yomhhlaba wethu kuhabhu yaseDubai idlala indawo ebaluleke kakhulu ukuqinisekisa ukungaphazamiseki kwesaplayi kwezempilo futhi siyajabula ukwesekwa esikunikiwe yi-United Arab Emirates ukusiza ishatha yokuqala bebhanoyi lalamasaplayi adingeka ngokushesha.
translations.hypothesis: लगायत प्रयासहरू, जुन लक्षित छ, यसले स्वास्थ्य अपरिहार्य, प्रतिक्रिया र लचीलापन, UAE, WHO, र साझेदारहरू, काम गरिरहेका छन्, ताकि वितरणको माध्यमले, पुग्नु, विश्वको सबैभन्दा कमजोर व्यक्तिहरू, कति आवश्यकता। डा. अहमद अल-मन्दाहरी, WHO क्षेत्रीय निर्देशक, पूर्वी भूमध्यसागरीय क्षेत्र, भन्छन्, "जागतिक स्वास्थ्य सङ्गठन, जारी रहन्छ, समन्वय, साथै सुडान स्वास्थ्य अधिकारीहरू, ताकि आवश्यक स्वास्थ्य सामग्रीहरू, मार्फत सबै उपलब्ध मार्गहरू। संघर्षको कारण, अस्पताल, औषधि, र प्राथमिक स्वास्थ्य सेवा, अभाव, जीवन रक्षक औषधिको। 30 टन, थप, तथा आवश्यक औषधिको, आगमन, WHO, 13 मुख्य स्वास्थ्य सेवा, र समर्थन, तथा सेवा, ती जो आवश्यक। हाम्रो जागतिक रसद केन्द्र, दुबई, महत्त्वपूर्ण भूमिका, सुनिश्चित, बिना, स्वास्थ्य सामान, र हामी आभारी, UAE, समर्थन, ताकि, हाम्रो पहिलो, यिन आवश्यक।

translations.source: It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.reference: Kungukweseka okunjalo njengoba lokhu kukhombisa ukwenza kombono wethu eSifundeni wezempilo kuwona wonke umuntu yiwona wonke umuntu. Usizo lwezokwelapha olunikezwe Inhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni nge-UAE kubalelwe kwafinyelela ku-55% isamba samasaplayi okwelapha anikezwe iSudan asuka phesheya ngo-2022, ukuveza isikhundla seUAE njengehabhu enkulu yosizo loluntu emazweni angaphandle.
translations.hypothesis: यस्तो समर्थन, जस्तै यो,ले देखाउँछ हाम्रो क्षेत्रीय दृष्टिकोण, iaitu "स्वास्थ्यको लागि सबै, द्वारा सबै।" चिकित्सा मद्दत प्रदान गरिएकोयसले, मार्फत विश्व स्वास्थ्य सङ्गठन (WHO) र यूएई, प्रतिनिधित्व गरेको लगभग 55% ले, कुल चिकित्सा आपूर्ति जुन 2022 मा सुडानलाई सेने,ले जनाउँछ, र यूएईको भूमिका अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय मानवीय मद्दतको मुख्य केन्द्र।

translations.source: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others.
translations.reference: Ukubambisana endaweni kubalulekile ekuqhubekeni emhlabeni okuya kokuhlobene nezempilo zamaSDG Umbiko wokuqhubeka omusha ukhishwe ngokuhlanganyela namhlanje kuma ejensi asayinile angu-13 eGlobal Action Plan for Helath Lives and Well being for All (SDG3 GAP). Umbiko usihloko esthi "What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?" uma kubhekwa iminyaka emine kusetshenzwa ndawonye ngama-ejensi ezindlela eziningi ezinkulu kuvezwa ukuthi yikuphi okusebenzile nokungasebenzanga ekuqinisweni ukubambisana nokwesekwa kwenqubeko yezwe okuuya nokuhlobene nezempilo zeSustainable Development Goals (amaSDG). Lo nyaka umaka iphuzu lahhafu eliya kumaSDG kodwa umhlaba usele ngemuva ukufika kuzinhloso zomhlaba. Kusukela ngokwethulwa kwayo ngo-2019, iSDG3 GAP yenze ukubambisana okusha kwezinhlaka phakathi kokusayina kwama ejensi ezindaweni ezibalulekile njengokugcineka kwezimali nokunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala kwabanye abantu.
translations.hypothesis: सहकार्य स्थानीय रूपमा महत्त्वपूर्ण हो, र यसले स्वास्थ्य-संबंधित SDGs मा पुगमा योगदान। आज, 13 हस्ताक्षर गर्ने सङ्गठनले "ग्लोबल ॲक्शन प्लान फर हेल्दी लाइभ्स एंड वेल-बीइंग फर ऑल (SDG3 GAP)" को संयुक्त रूपमा नयाँ प्रगति रिपोर्ट जारी गर्दै छन्। रिपोर्ट, "के सफल भयो? के असफल भयो? के आउछ?" ले चार वर्षको संयुक्त कार्यले दर्शाएका छन्, र यसले सहयोग र समर्थनलाई बढावा दिन र देशहरूलाई स्वास्थ्य-संबंधित स्थायी विकास लक्ष्य (SDGs) मा पुगमा मद्दत गरेको छ। यस वर्ष, SDGs को आधा मार्गमा छ, तर दुनयले पुग्ने लक्ष्यमा पछि छ। 2019 मा यसको प्रक्षेपण पछि, SDG3 GAP हस्ताक्षर गर्ने सङ्गठनहरू बीच, स्थायी वित्तपोषण र प्राथमिक स्वास्थ्य सेवा जस्ता मुख्य क्षेत्रहरूमा नयाँ सहकार्य संरचनाहरूको सिर्जनाको मद्दत गरेको छ।

translations.source: At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.reference: Okungenani amazwe angu-67 akhulumile kanye noma ngaphezulu ngeSDG3 GAP ukuqhuba izingqikithi. Senze inqubeko ebalulekile, kodwa kusanendlela ende okumele siyihambe ukwenza ngcono indlela izinhlangano eziningi ezisebenzisana ngayo ukweseka amazwe. Kumele silalele esikutshelwa ngamazwe futhi silandele imiyalelo,kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO noSihlalo weSDG3 GAP kuzinhloko zeqoqo. "Ngibonga ababambisani ngokubambisana kwazo nokuzihlola ngokwethembeka okuqukethwe kulombiko. "Umbiko unikeza iziphakamiso eziyisithupha ezibalulekile ukuqinisekisa ukuthi amazwe ayazuza ekwesekweni okukhishiwe nokubona ukuzibophezela okwenziwe kuSDG3 GAP.
